sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The GitHub connector syncs repositories, issues, and pull requests from GitHub into the Shielva knowledge base using the GitHub REST API v3. It supports Personal Access Tokens (classic or fine-grained) and GitHub App tokens via Bearer authentication. The connector implements Link-header pagination, X-RateLimit-Remaining checks, exponential backoff retry, and a circuit breaker for reliable, throttle-safe data ingestion.

subsections.title: Personal Access Token (Classic)
subsections.content: The simplest authentication method. Create a PAT under GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic). Grant the repo scope (and read:org for organization access). The token begins with ghp_.
steps: Log in to GitHub and go to Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic).
Select scopes: repo (full repository access) and read:org (if syncing org repos).
Click Generate token and copy it — GitHub shows it only once.
Paste the token into the access_token field in Shielva.

subsections.title: Fine-grained PAT (Enhanced Security)
subsections.content: For least-privilege access. Create under Developer settings → Fine-grained tokens. Grant read-only permissions for Metadata, Contents, Issues, and Pull requests.
steps: Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens.
Under Repository access choose All repositories or specific repos.
Under Permissions grant: Metadata (read), Contents (read), Issues (read), Pull requests (read).
Paste the token into the access_token field in Shielva.

fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) or fine-grained PAT. Required for all API calls.

fields.label: Organization Name
fields.description: Optional GitHub organization slug. When set, repositories are fetched from GET /orgs/{org}/repos instead of GET /user/repos.

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content: The sync() method performs a full sync in three phases: (1) fetch all repositories (user-level or org-level); (2) for each repository, fetch open issues (excluding PRs returned by the /issues endpoint); (3) for each repository, fetch open pull requests. Each raw API record is normalized into a ConnectorDocument with a stable SHA-256[:16] source_id and optionally ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

methods.description: Validates credentials by calling GET /user. Returns HEALTHY + CONNECTED on success, OFFLINE + MISSING_CREDENTIALS when access_token is absent, OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS on 401/403.
Pings GET /user and returns HEALTHY (with username), DEGRADED (transient network error), or OFFLINE (auth failure or circuit open). Updates the circuit breaker state.

methods.signature: async def sync(full: bool = False, since: datetime | None = None, kb_id: str = '') -> SyncResult
methods.description: Full paginated sync of repos, issues, and PRs. Returns documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed, and SyncStatus (COMPLETED / PARTIAL / FAILED).

methods.signature: async def list_repos(org: str | None = None) -> list[dict]
methods.description: List all repositories. Uses GET /orgs/{org}/repos when org is provided, otherwise GET /user/repos.

methods.signature: async def list_issues(owner: str, repo: str, state: str = 'open') -> list[dict]
methods.description: List issues for a repository filtered by state. Paginates via Link header.

methods.signature: async def get_issue(owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> dict
methods.description: Retrieve a single issue by number.

methods.signature: async def list_pull_requests(owner: str, repo: str, state: str = 'open') -> list[dict]
methods.description: List pull requests for a repository filtered by state. Paginates via Link header.

methods.signature: async def get_pull_request(owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> dict
methods.description: Retrieve a single pull request by number.
Closes the underlying httpx.AsyncClient and clears the http_client reference.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Every GitHub entity is normalized into a ConnectorDocument. The source_id is a stable 16-character hex prefix of SHA-256(entity_type + ':' + html_url).

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited key-value representation of the entity's properties. Issue/PR body appended.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Entity-type-specific properties. Always includes entity_type key.

exceptions.name: GitHubError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, and code.

exceptions.name: GitHubAuthError
exceptions.description: Raised on 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden. Not retried.

exceptions.name: GitHubRateLimitError
exceptions.description: Raised on 429 Too Many Requests or when X-RateLimit-Remaining reaches 0. Carries retry_after (seconds). Retried after the specified delay.

exceptions.name: GitHubNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Raised on 404 Not Found. Carries resource name and resource ID.

exceptions.name: GitHubNetworkError
exceptions.description: Raised on httpx.TimeoutException or httpx.NetworkError. Retried with exponential backoff.

exceptions.name: GitHubServerError
exceptions.description: Raised on 5xx responses from GitHub. Retried.

items.cause: Personal Access Token is invalid, expired, or was revoked.
items.solution: Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens, regenerate the token, and update the connector's access_token.

items.cause: The PAT is missing the repo or read:org scope.
items.solution: Edit the token in GitHub, add the required scopes (repo for repositories/issues/PRs; read:org for organization repos), and regenerate.

items.cause: The org field is set to a slug that does not exist or the token lacks read:org permission.
items.solution: Verify the organization slug (lowercase, hyphenated) and that the token has read:org scope.

items.cause: GitHub rate limit exhausted (5,000 req/hr for PATs).
items.solution: The connector retries automatically. For large organizations consider running syncs during off-peak hours or using a GitHub App token (15,000 req/hr).

items.cause: Transient network errors have tripped the circuit breaker (5 consecutive failures).
items.solution: Resolve the underlying network or auth issue, then trigger a health check to reset the circuit breaker.

items.cause: One or more API records returned unexpected data causing normalization to fail.
items.solution: Check connector logs for which html_url values failed. Inspect those records on GitHub for corrupted or null property values.
